questions.question: 1 (a) Pour approximately 3 cm 3 of water into the boiling tube. Measure the height h 0 of the water level from the bench, as shown in Fig. 1.1. h 0 = ......................................................... cm Suggest one precaution that is taken when measuring the height of the water level to ensure the reading is accurate. parts.answer: 5  h values increasing, smallest > h • axes labelled with quantity and unit • appropriate scales (occupying at least ½ grid) • plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots • well-judged line and thin line water volumes small – large (%) uncertainty test-tube diameter small – large (%) uncertainty in answer owtte height changes small so unreliable October/November 2022 Question Answer Marks

 for beaker A decreasing  for beaker B decreasing and all temperatures recorded to at least 1°C  for beaker B decreasing less quickly than beaker A comparison of temperature changes over 180 s, matching statement (need to see values used in justification) 1 A > x B from correct calculations 1 other appropriate suggestion initial / starting temperature/ volume of water / room temperature A and suitable explanation 1 October/November 2022 Question Answer Marks I T < 1.00 (A) to at least 2dps PQ with 2 or more sig figs S > V 1 R S to either 2 or 3 sig figs 1

I P + I Q within 10% of I T units correct throughout A, V,  with values / comparative values seen and ‘within limits of experimental accuracy’ / owtte value of I P + I Q seen, and I T seen and X on series part of circuit less straightforward to change current / more difficult to obtain a greater number of values October/November 2022 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 apparatus: thermometer, metre rule MP2 method: heat ball in water and measure temperature drop from measure height ball bounces to MP3 repeat for new temperature 1 MP4 control variable: height of drop MP5 table: columns with units for temperature and Bounce Height. MP6 analysis: compare readings in the table to see if change in temperature produces change in dependent variable / plot line graph of Temperature v Bounce Height MP7 additional point (one from): keep in water until sure whole ball at same temp as water use of water bath at least 5 sets of data taken, repeat each measurement and take average, repeat experiment for different variation (e.g. different bounce surface / height of drop) same ball / diameter of ball, bounce surface / type of floor.
